dashboard_recap.headline: MLB Daily Recap - May 15, 2026
dashboard_recap.article:
**Phillies Blast Past Pirates in Thrilling 11-9 Victory**

In a high-scoring affair, the Philadelphia Phillies edged out the Pittsburgh Pirates 11-9, thanks in large part to Kyle Schwarber's dominant performance. The slugger went 3-for-5 with two home runs and five RBIs, leading the Phillies' charge at the plate. José Alvarado also earned the win, tossing a scoreless inning with three strikeouts. The Pirates' Marcell Ozuna and Oneil Cruz also had strong games, but ultimately fell short.

**Rays Shut Down Marlins in 7-2 Rout**

Meanwhile, the Tampa Bay Rays dominated the Miami Marlins 7-2, behind a strong outing from Jesse Scholtens. The right-hander pitched five innings, allowing just one run on five hits while striking out four. Cedric Mullins was the Rays' top hitter, going 4-for-4 with a home run and an RBI. The Marlins' Xavier Edwards and Connor Norby also had solid games, but it wasn't enough to prevent the Rays' victory.

**Yankees Take Down Mets in 5-2 Win**

In another one-run game, the New York Yankees edged out the New York Mets 5-2. Cam Schlittler was the star of the show, pitching 6.2 innings and allowing just one run on two hits while striking out nine. Ben Rice also had a strong game, going 3-for-5 with a home run and an RBI. The Mets' Brett Baty and Juan Soto also had good outings, but it wasn't enough to prevent the Yankees' win.

**Diamondbacks Rout Rockies 9-1**

The Arizona Diamondbacks took care of the Colorado Rockies 9-1, behind a strong outing from Merrill Kelly. The right-hander pitched nine innings, allowing just one run on four hits while striking out three. Hunter Goodman was the Diamondbacks' top hitter, going 1-for-4 with a home run and an RBI. The Rockies' Corbin Carroll and Ildemaro Vargas also had solid games, but ultimately fell short.

**Dodgers Shut Down Angels 6-0**

The Los Angeles Dodgers completed a sweep of the Los Angeles Angels 6-0, behind a strong outing from Edgardo Henriquez. The right-hander earned the win, pitching six innings and allowing no runs on no hits while striking out three. Andy Pages was the Dodgers' top hitter, going 1-for-4 with a home run and three RBIs. The Angels' Teoscar Hernández also had a strong game, going 1-for-3 with a home run.

**Padres Shut Down Mariners 2-0**

The San Diego Padres took care of the Seattle Mariners 2-0, behind a strong outing from Randy Vásquez. The right-hander earned the win, pitching six innings and allowing just one run on five hits while striking out six. Miguel Andujar was the Padres' top hitter, going 2-for-4 with an RBI. The Mariners' Sung-Mun Song and Ramón Laureano also had solid games, but ultimately fell short.

**Athletics Take Down Giants 5-2**

The Oakland Athletics completed a sweep of the San Francisco Giants 5-2, behind a strong outing from Aaron Civale. The right-hander earned the win, pitching seven innings and allowing just two runs on five hits while striking out five. Luis Arraez was the Athletics' top hitter, going 4-for-5 with a home run and an RBI. The Giants' Henry Bolte and Shea Langeliers also had solid games, but ultimately fell short.
